title: Mortality rate, infant (per 1,000 live births)
definition: Infant mortality rate is the number of infants dying before reaching one year of age, per 1,000 live births in a given year.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.shortHook: A child-health map with unmistakable low and high anchors.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include South Sudan, Nigeria, Niger, while the lightest include Estonia, Belarus, Japan; that contrast is the fastest way to read the infant mortality pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Infant mortality is a compact signal of maternal health, medical access, and living conditions.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Under-5 mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Neonatal mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Life expectancy can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Practice only so players can learn the distinction.
